mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output and order books; this translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months reflects deteriorating household income and aggregate demand; this contractionary impulse reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when the trigger condition is met over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with falling industrial production (INDPRO) constitutes a stagflationary tightening regime; the joint effect raises real borrowing costs while output contracts, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month while INDPRO falls month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS change and INDPRO change does not show a statistically significant negative coefficient (p < 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) sustained above 5% restricts credit availability, elevates discount rates, and crowds out private investment; this financial compression produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered observations during identified tightening cycles over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) captures robust consumer expenditure flows; stronger top-line revenues for consumer-facing firms translate into positive earnings surprises and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following monthly RSAFS growth are not positive in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reveal broad-based demand collapse; the interaction deepens recession probability, tightens financial conditions, and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month and RSAFS declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of HOUST and RSAFS declines fails to predict negative 60-day returns with Rank IC < -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% reflects acute manufacturing contraction and involuntary inventory accumulation; this supply-demand imbalance cuts corporate pricing power and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month change in payroll employment (PAYEMS) validates aggregate demand expansion; rising employment expands household income and consumption, lifting corporate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.
